sections.content:
Let me continue exploring the UP cycle. I've seen 5 different symbols so far. Let me continue to see if there are more and if it eventually cycles back. But I've already used 9 actions out of ~54, so I need to be more efficient. Let me continue the cycle to understand it fully, then figure out the goal.
Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The pink symbols seem to cycle through the 6 pink top tiles. Let me check:
- Initial: Tile (1,1) pattern
- UP #1: Tile (2,3) pattern
- UP #2: Tile (0,1) pattern (BBBBP/BPPBB/BPPPB/BBPPB/PBBBB)
- UP #3: Tile (0,3) pattern (BBBBP/BPPBP/BPPBB/BPPBP/BBBBP) - wait, that's not quite right
- UP #4: Tile (1,3) pattern (PPBBB/PPBPB/BBBBB/BPBPP/BBBPP) - need to check
- UP #5: Tile (1,1) again? No, let me re-check.
Actually, I should just continue cycling to see the full set and when it repeats. But I need to be more careful about matching. Let me compare the current symbols to the top tiles.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Continue UP cycle and compare to top tiles
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

# Extract all top tiles as patterns (just B and P, dots for S/g)
tile_rows = [range(5, 10), range(14, 19), range(23, 28)]
tile_cols = [range(13, 18), range(23, 28), range(36, 41), range(46, 51)]

top_tiles = {}
for tr_idx, tr in enumerate(tile_rows):
    for tc_idx, tc in enumerate(tile_cols):
        pattern = []
        for r in tr:
            crop = ascii_lines[r][min(tc):max(tc)+1]
            simplified = ''.join(['.' if c in 'gGS' else c for c in crop])
            pattern.append(simplified)
        top_tiles[(tr_idx, tc_idx)] = pattern

# Extract current pink symbol 0
pink_sym = []
for r in range(52, 57):
    crop = ascii_lines[r][15:21]
    # Normalize: first 5 chars (ignore trailing P)
    simplified = crop[:5]
    pink_sym.append(simplified)

print("Current pink sym 0 (normalized):")
for row in pink_sym:
    print(f"  {row}")

# Compare to all pink tiles (odd column indices)
print("\nMatching against pink tiles:")
for (tr, tc), pattern in top_tiles.items():
    if tc % 2 == 1:  # pink tiles are in odd columns
        match = all(pink_sym[r] == pattern[r] for r in range(5))
        if match:
            print(f"  MATCH: Tile ({tr},{tc})")
        else:
            print(f"  No match: Tile ({tr},{tc})")
            for r in range(5):
                if pink_sym[r] != pattern[r]:
                    print(f"    Row {r}: sym={pink_sym[r]} tile={pattern[r]}")

# Continue cycling to see all 6 pink tiles
print("\n--- Continuing UP cycle ---")
for i in range(6):
    action(['UP'])
    ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
    pink_sym = []
    for r in range(52, 57):
        pink_sym.append(ascii_lines[r][15:20])
    
    for (tr, tc), pattern in top_tiles.items():
        if tc % 2 == 1:
            match = all(pink_sym[r] == pattern[r] for r in range(5))
            if match:
                print(f"  After additional UP #{i+1}: matches Tile ({tr},{tc})")
                break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
